---
title: "Group 3 QIIME2 Workflow"
author: "Simar Jawanda"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  eval = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

# Overview

This file documents the QIIME2 workflow used for Group 3. Group 3 contains the paired-end BioProject `PRJNA831840`.

The workbook notes say that primer-testing commands were run only for exploration and were not part of the final retained workflow. Those trial commands are therefore not reproduced here.

# Step 1: Set Project Directory and QIIME2 Container

```bash
PROJ="/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_3_PRJNA831840"; cd "$PROJ"
docker pull quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1
qiime_docker () { docker run --rm -it --platform linux/amd64 --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v "$PROJ:/data" -w /data quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1 qiime "$@"; }
```

# Step 2: Prepare FASTQ Files and Manifest

```bash
cd "$PROJ/fastq"; for f in SRR*.fastq; do [ -e "$f" ] || continue; s="${f%.fastq}"; gzip -c "$f" > "$s".fastq.gz; done; cd "$PROJ"
mkdir -p manifests
{
  printf "sample-id\tforward-absolute-filepath\treverse-absolute-filepath\n"
  for f in "$PROJ"/fastq/*_1.fastq.gz; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    s="$(basename "$f" _1.fastq.gz)"
    r="$PROJ/fastq/${s}_2.fastq.gz"
    [ -e "$r" ] || { echo "Missing reverse read for $s" >&2; continue; }
    printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n" "$s" "/data/fastq/${s}_1.fastq.gz" "/data/fastq/${s}_2.fastq.gz"
  done | sort -V
} > manifests/pe.tsv
head -n 5 manifests/pe.tsv | cat -vet
```

# Step 3: Import Reads and Summarize Demultiplexed Data

```bash
qiime_docker tools import \
  --type 'SampleData[PairedEndSequencesWithQuality]' \
  --input-path manifests/pe.tsv \
  --input-format PairedEndFastqManifestPhred33V2 \
  --output-path reads.qza

qiime_docker demux summarize \
  --i-data reads.qza \
  --o-visualization reads.qzv
```

# Step 4: Proceed Without Additional Primer Trimming

The retained workflow did not include a final `cutadapt` trimming step because the tested primer pairs all failed to match the deposited reads. The workbook notes state that this was interpreted as evidence that primer sequences had likely already been trimmed before SRA deposition.

The quality plots from `reads.qzv` were then used directly to choose DADA2 truncation lengths.

# Step 5: Run DADA2 and Retain the Final Truncation Choice

```bash
qiime_docker dada2 denoise-paired \
  --i-demultiplexed-seqs reads.qza \
  --p-trim-left-f 0 \
  --p-trim-left-r 0 \
  --p-trunc-len-f 234 \
  --p-trunc-len-r 152 \
  --p-max-ee-f 2 \
  --p-max-ee-r 2 \
  --o-table table_234_152.qza \
  --o-representative-sequences rep-seqs_234_152.qza \
  --o-denoising-stats denoising-stats_234_152.qza \
  --o-base-transition-stats base-transition-stats_234_152.qza

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file denoising-stats_234_152.qza \
  --o-visualization denoising-stats_234_152.qzv

qiime_docker dada2 denoise-paired \
  --i-demultiplexed-seqs reads.qza \
  --p-trim-left-f 0 \
  --p-trim-left-r 0 \
  --p-trunc-len-f 224 \
  --p-trunc-len-r 142 \
  --p-max-ee-f 2 \
  --p-max-ee-r 2 \
  --o-table table_224_142.qza \
  --o-representative-sequences rep-seqs_224_142.qza \
  --o-denoising-stats denoising-stats_224_142.qza \
  --o-base-transition-stats base-transition-stats_224_142.qza

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file denoising-stats_224_142.qza \
  --o-visualization denoising-stats_224_142.qzv
```

Final DADA2 note:

- `234/152` was tested first.
- `224/142` was retained because it gave better values while still preserving strong hypothetical overlap and following the 10 bp buffer rule.

# Step 6: Convert Metadata for QIIME2

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import csv

infile = "metadata.csv"
outfile = "metadata.tsv"

with open(infile, newline="", encoding="utf-8-sig") as fin:
    rows = list(csv.reader(fin))

if not rows:
    raise SystemExit("metadata.csv is empty")

rows[0][0] = "#Sample ID"

with open(outfile, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as fout:
    writer = csv.writer(fout, delimiter="\t", lineterminator="\n")
    writer.writerows(rows)

print(f"Wrote {outfile}")
PY

python3 - <<'PY'
import csv
p="metadata.tsv"
rows=list(csv.reader(open(p), delimiter="\t"))
hdr=rows[0]
hdr=[("orig_ID" if h=="ID" else h) for h in hdr]
rows[0]=hdr
with open(p,"w",newline="") as f:
    csv.writer(f,delimiter="\t",lineterminator="\n").writerows(rows)
print("Renamed column 'ID' -> 'orig_ID' in metadata.tsv")
PY
```

# Step 7: Remove the Kit Control Sample

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table filter-samples \
  --i-table table_224_142.qza \
  --m-metadata-file metadata.tsv \
  --p-where "[#Sample ID] != 'SRR18914140'" \
  --o-filtered-table table_no_control.qza
```

Filter note: `SRR18914140` was removed as the kit control sample.

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table table_no_control.qza \
  --o-feature-frequencies feature-frequencies_no_control.qza \
  --o-sample-frequencies sample-frequencies_no_control.qza \
  --o-summary table_no_control_summary.qzv

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data rep-seqs_224_142.qza \
  --o-visualization rep-seqs_224_142.qzv
```

# Step 8: Filter Low-Abundance Features and Assign Taxonomy

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table filter-features \
  --i-table table_no_control.qza \
  --p-min-frequency 33 \
  --p-min-samples 2 \
  --o-filtered-table table_filtered.qza

qiime_docker feature-table filter-seqs \
  --i-data rep-seqs_224_142.qza \
  --i-table table_filtered.qza \
  --o-filtered-data rep-seqs_filtered.qza

qiime_docker feature-classifier classify-consensus-vsearch \
  --i-query rep-seqs_filtered.qza \
  --i-reference-reads silva-138-99-seqs.qza \
  --i-reference-taxonomy silva-138-99-tax.qza \
  --p-perc-identity 0.97 \
  --p-query-cov 0.8 \
  --p-threads 8 \
  --o-classification taxonomy.qza \
  --o-search-results taxonomy_hits.qza

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file taxonomy.qza \
  --o-visualization taxonomy.qzv
```

Filtering note: the workbook recorded a calculated cutoff of `32.655`, which was implemented as `33`.

# Step 9: Remove Mitochondria and Chloroplast Features

```bash
qiime_docker taxa filter-table \
  --i-table table_filtered.qza \
  --i-taxonomy taxonomy.qza \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-table table_no_mc.qza

qiime_docker taxa filter-seqs \
  --i-sequences rep-seqs_filtered.qza \
  --i-taxonomy taxonomy.qza \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-sequences rep-seqs_no_mc.qza

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data rep-seqs_no_mc.qza \
  --o-visualization rep-seqs_no_mc.qzv
```

Verification note: the first five filtered sequences were checked by BLAST and matched microbial sequences.

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table table_no_mc.qza \
  --m-metadata-file metadata.tsv \
  --o-feature-frequencies table-no-mc-feature-freq.qza \
  --o-sample-frequencies table-no-mc-sample-freq.qza \
  --o-summary table-no-mc.qzv
```

# Step 10: Collapse to Genus Level and Export Final Outputs

```bash
qiime_docker taxa collapse \
  --i-table table_no_mc.qza \
  --i-taxonomy taxonomy.qza \
  --p-level 6 \
  --o-collapsed-table table-genus.qza

qiime_docker feature-table relative-frequency \
  --i-table table-genus.qza \
  --o-relative-frequency-table table-genus-relfreq.qza

qiime_docker taxa barplot \
  --i-table table_no_mc.qza \
  --i-taxonomy taxonomy.qza \
  --m-metadata-file metadata.tsv \
  --o-visualization taxa-barplot-no-mc.qzv

conda activate biom_env

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path table-genus-relfreq.qza \
  --output-path exported-genus-relfreq

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path taxonomy.qza \
  --output-path exported-taxonomy

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path table_no_mc.qza \
  --output-path exported-asv-table

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path rep-seqs_no_mc.qza \
  --output-path exported-rep-seqs

biom convert \
  -i exported-genus-relfreq/feature-table.biom \
  -o exported-genus-relfreq/genus-relfreq.tsv \
  --to-tsv

biom convert \
  -i exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom \
  -o exported-asv-table/asv-table.tsv \
  --to-tsv

awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"} NR==1{print "#OTUID","taxonomy"; next} {print $1,$2}' \
exported-taxonomy/taxonomy.tsv > exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv

biom add-metadata \
  -i exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom \
  -o exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom \
  --observation-metadata-fp exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv \
  --sc-separated taxonomy

biom convert \
  -i exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom \
  -o exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.tsv \
  --to-tsv \
  --header-key taxonomy

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path denoising-stats_224_142.qza \
  --output-path exported-denoising-stats

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path reads.qza \
  --output-path exported-reads
```
